m:Sender.c:Name: Statistische Amt der Europäischen Union (Eurostat)
Statistical Office of the European Union (Eurostat)
Office de statistique de l'Union européenne (Eurostat)

c:Annotation.c:AnnotationText: Combined code list, contains standard codes and codes not associated with the concept of the code list
Eurostat Standard Code Lists
classification: 'Activity Coding List for harmonised European time use surveys, 2000'
Not derived

s:Codelist.c:Name: Classification of activities for time use
Klassifikation der Aktivitäten für Zeitverwendungen
Classification des activités pour l'emploi du temps
s:Codelist.c:Description: Classification of activities for time use is a three-level hierarchical classification, composed of major divisions, divisions and groups of all possible activities undertaken by the general population during the 24 hours that make up the day. The Activity Coding List is used in the Harmonised European Time Use Surveys (HETUS) to quantify how much time people spend on various activities, including paid work, household chores and family care, personal care, voluntary work, social life, travel and leisure.
